title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder in alleged group attack on Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez, 25, in Chelsea.
Incident occurred March 19 near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue; Lopez died from multiple stab wounds in the hospital.
Police found blood-stained clothing, a knife, and machete; Castellanos Cerrato held without bail, returns to court April 21.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston metro area saw a slowdown in population growth in 2025, marking the lowest since the COVID-19 pandemic.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,524 from 2024 to 2025, with Middlesex County seeing the largest county growth.
Factors contributing to slowed growth include high taxes, housing costs, and significant domestic out-migration from the state.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Marineland Right Whale Project launched a GoFundMe for North Atlantic right whale conservation, raising $630 of a $15,000 target.
Donations support research, including surveys and equipment, critical for protecting the critically endangered North Atlantic right whales.
North Atlantic right whales face risks like vessel strikes and entanglement; recent deaths highlight the need for continued conservation efforts.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Patriots acquired Randy Moss from the Raiders in 2007; he set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a season.
Wes Welker was traded to the Patriots from the Dolphins in 2007, becoming one of the top slot receivers in the NFL.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, showing potential but facing issues; he is the only one to win a Super Bowl ring.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning for disputing a strike three call on Trevor Story's check swing.
Home plate umpire C.B. Bucknor called Story out, not deferring to the first base umpire, leading to Cora's ejection.
The Red Sox lost a crucial ABS challenge early, affecting later opportunities to contest umpire calls in a 6-5 loss.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story disagreed with umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call during Red Sox's 6-5 loss against the Twins.
Manager Alex Cora was ejected after arguing Bucknor's decision not to consult the first base umpire.
The Red Sox are now 1-1 for the season, unable to hold onto late-game momentum after tying the score.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals in 16 games since returning from the Olympic break post-concussion for the Boston Bruins.
Zacha missed the Olympic Games due to an ill-timed concussion but has become a key player for the Bruins afterward.
Zacha's trade from New Jersey has paid off, reaching a career-high 26 goals and close to surpassing his points record.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray's Red Sox debut ended with a no-decision in a 6-5 loss to the Cincinnati Reds.
Gray allowed four runs over four innings, including a solo home run by Sal Stewart in the third inning.
Manager Alex Cora cited defensive issues and praised Gray's settling in, while the bullpen held strong after his departure.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 people gathered at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' rally, part of a nationwide protest.
The event included speeches from Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, focusing on political and social issues.
Protesters participated in 3,300 rallies nationwide, with Massachusetts hosting over 160, the second-highest number in the US.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set at Boston's House of Blues, featuring 17 songs and theatrical shock rock elements.
The heavy metal band incorporated social and political satire, targeting figures like Donald Trump and Kristi Noem during their show.
The concert's encore included a cover of Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club,' dedicated to late band member Dave Brockie.
